book: The Oil Kings_ How the U - Cooper, Andrew Scott.kfx

issues.description: Photographic montage at top shows slight sepia toning inconsistency between left and right portrait images, though this may be intentional design
issues.suggestion: Verify source cover image fidelity; if consistent with original design, no action needed

issues.description: Copyright page text runs multiple distinct data fields together without line breaks (publisher address, copyright notice, edition info all merged into two run-on lines at top)
issues.suggestion: Ensure copyright page metadata is broken into separate lines or paragraphs matching original print layout

issues.description: Library of Congress Cataloging-in-Publication Data block merges bibliographic fields into continuous prose without standard line breaks between entries
issues.suggestion: Preserve line-break formatting in CIP block to match standard bibliographic display

issues.description: Printer's key sequence '1 3 5 7 9 10 8 6 4 2' runs inline with 'Designed by Ruth Lee-Mui Manufactured in the United States of America' without separation
issues.suggestion: Add line break before printer's key sequence to match standard copyright page formatting

issues.description: TOC page shows only the 'CONTENTS' heading with no entries — the entire table of contents body is missing. All chapter titles, page numbers, and front/back matter entries are absent
issues.suggestion: Re-run conversion ensuring TOC entries are extracted and rendered; check if TOC data was lost during EPUB generation or if NCX/Nav document is empty

issues.description: Page is almost entirely blank white space below the heading, indicating a content rendering failure rather than intentional design
issues.suggestion: Investigate whether TOC HTML content was generated but not rendered, or if source TOC was not parsed correctly

issues.description: Chapter heading 'Chapter Four CONTINGENCIES' renders chapter ordinal and chapter title in the same font weight and size with no visual separation — the word 'CONTINGENCIES' is not differentiated from 'Chapter Four' as a distinct title element
issues.suggestion: Apply separate styling to chapter number label vs. chapter title text, e.g., chapter number in regular weight and title in bold or larger size

issues.description: Epigraph block (two quotes attributed to The Shah and Henry Kissinger) runs as a single merged paragraph without visual separation between the two distinct quotations
issues.suggestion: Add paragraph break or em-dash separator between the two epigraph quotes to clarify they are distinct attributions

issues.description: Ellipsis formatting uses '. . .' (spaced periods) consistently, which is a valid typographic style but may indicate source formatting carried over literally rather than using proper ellipsis character
issues.suggestion: Verify ellipsis character rendering is intentional style choice from original text; if so, no action needed

issues.description: Chapter heading 'Chapter Eleven ROYAL FLUSH' renders chapter ordinal and title in the same line with identical weight/size, matching the same issue seen on page 119 — no visual hierarchy between label and title
issues.suggestion: Apply consistent fix across all chapter headings to differentiate chapter number from chapter title typographically

issues.description: Epigraph block merges two distinct quotes (Ford 1976 and The Shah 1976) into a single paragraph without a line break between them
issues.suggestion: Separate epigraph quotations into distinct paragraphs to clarify attribution boundaries

issues.description: Spaced ellipsis style '. . .' used throughout quoted material, consistent with page 229 — likely intentional authorial/editorial style
issues.suggestion: No action needed if this matches source document style

issues.description: Endnotes entries are split mid-citation across paragraphs in multiple instances: '313 Alan Greenspan, had gone before Congress: Edwin L. Dale Jr., "Economy Will' ends as a paragraph, then 'Outperform Forecast, Greenspan Says,"' begins a new non-italic paragraph — the citation is broken across a paragraph boundary
issues.suggestion: Ensure endnote citation text is not split across paragraph elements; each note entry should be a single unbroken block

issues.description: Entry for '314 the steepest fall in consumer retail spending' citation is split: italic text ends with 'Last Month to' then '$52.64 Billion,' Wall Street Journal...' continues as a separate non-italic paragraph, breaking the citation
issues.suggestion: Merge split citation paragraphs; check source HTML for erroneous paragraph tags inserted mid-sentence in notes section

issues.description: Entry for '314 "Temporary pauses of this kind aren't uncommon"' is split: italic text ends mid-citation with 'Burns Is Optimistic About the' and continues in a new non-italic paragraph — same pattern of broken citations
issues.suggestion: Audit entire endnotes section for mid-citation paragraph splits; likely a systematic extraction artifact from source PDF

issues.description: Entry for '314 "unsuitable entertainment"' citation split: italic text ends with 'The Times of My Life (New' and resumes in separate non-italic paragraph 'York: Harper & Row, 1978), 223.'
issues.suggestion: Same fix as above — endnote paragraph splitting is a systematic issue throughout the notes section requiring global remediation

issues.description: Italic formatting for note citation lead-ins (page number + quoted phrase) appears correct, but the alternation between italic and non-italic across broken paragraphs creates visual confusion about what is quoted vs. bibliographic
issues.suggestion: Once paragraph splits are resolved, verify italic scoping covers only the intended quoted text portions

summary: Evaluated 8 pages across 1 batch(es). Average page score: 76/100.
